defs: One who arbitrates a duel; a sidesman to a fencer; a second; an umpire.
One who pertinaciously contends for some trifling things, as a point of etiquette; an unreasonable, obstinate contender; as, a stickler for ceremony.
quotes: Basilius, the judge, appointed sticklers and trumpets whom the others should obey.
Our former chiefs, like sticklers of the war, First sought to inflame the parties, then to poise.
The Tory or High-church were the greatest sticklers against the exorbitant proceedings of King James II.
